questions.question: What is the Magma category designation for elliptic curves?
questions.explanation: All elliptic curves in Magma belong to the category CrvEll.

questions.question: What is the generalised Weierstrass equation used to define an elliptic curve in Magma?
options: y² + a1xy + a3y = x³ + a2x² + a4x + a6
questions.explanation: The full generalised Weierstrass form is y² + a1xy + a3y = x³ + a2x² + a4x + a6.

questions.question: When EllipticCurve([a, b]) is used with a 2-element sequence, what curve is defined?
questions.explanation: A 2-element sequence [a, b] creates the short Weierstrass curve y² = x³ + ax + b, assuming a1 = a2 = a3 = 0.

questions.question: What is the type of points on an elliptic curve in Magma?
questions.explanation: Points on elliptic curves have type PtEll and lie in point sets of type SetPtEll.

questions.question: What curve does EllipticCurveWithjInvariant(1728) return?
questions.explanation: For j = 1728, Magma returns the curve y² = x³ + x.

questions.question: What happens if the discriminant of the supplied Weierstrass coefficients is zero?
options: Magma issues a warning and returns the singular curve
The construction fails because the curve must be nonsingular
Magma automatically adjusts the coefficients to make it nonsingular
A general scheme is returned instead of an elliptic curve
questions.explanation: A nonzero discriminant is required; if the discriminant is zero the curve is singular and the construction fails.

questions.question: What Magma function tests whether a sequence of ring elements defines a valid elliptic curve?
questions.explanation: IsEllipticCurve(s) returns true (and the curve) if the discriminant is nonzero, false otherwise.

questions.question: Which function returns an isomorphic curve in simplified Weierstrass form y² = x³ + ax + b?
questions.explanation: WeierstrassModel(E) returns a simplified Weierstrass form, provided the base field does not have characteristic 2 or 3.

questions.question: How does ChangeRing(E, K) differ from BaseExtend(E, K)?
options: ChangeRing only works for finite fields
ChangeRing uses standard coercion and works even when K is not an extension of the base field
ChangeRing also returns the isomorphism between the two curves
ChangeRing only changes the base ring if the two fields are isomorphic
questions.explanation: ChangeRing uses standard coercion, making it useful when there is no ring homomorphism (e.g. from Q to a finite field), unlike BaseExtend which requires K to extend the base field.

questions.question: What does MinimalModel(E) guarantee for a curve E over Q?
options: The curve has the fewest rational points
The discriminant has minimal p-adic valuation at every prime p
The curve is in short Weierstrass form
questions.explanation: A global minimal model minimises the p-adic valuation of the discriminant simultaneously at all primes.

questions.question: What does QuadraticTwist(E) return when E is defined over a finite field?
options: A nonisomorphic curve whose trace is the negation of Trace(E)
All quadratic twists of E as a sequence
The twist of E by the characteristic of the field
questions.explanation: Over a finite field, QuadraticTwist(E) with no second argument returns the unique nonisomorphic quadratic twist, whose trace is -Trace(E).

questions.question: What does IsTwist(E, F) test?
options: Whether E and F are isomorphic over the base field
Whether E and F are isomorphic over some extension field (via their j-invariants)
Whether F is specifically a quadratic twist of E
Whether E and F have the same number of rational points
questions.explanation: IsTwist only compares j-invariants; equal j-invariants mean the curves become isomorphic over an extension field.

questions.question: When creating an elliptic curve from polynomials EllipticCurve(f, h), what must be true of f?
options: f must be monic of degree 4 and h must have degree at most 2
f must be monic of degree 3 and h must have degree at most 1
f must have degree 2 and h must have degree 3
f and h must both be monic of degree 3
questions.explanation: f must be monic of degree 3 and h must have degree at most 1; the curve is then y² + h(x)y = f(x).

questions.question: What Magma function returns a representative supersingular elliptic curve over a finite field K?
questions.explanation: SupersingularEllipticCurve(K) returns a representative supersingular elliptic curve over the finite field K.

questions.question: What is the Magma category for subgroup schemes of elliptic curves?
questions.explanation: Subgroup schemes of elliptic curves belong to the category SchGrpEll.

questions.question: What does BaseExtend(E, n) do when E is defined over a finite field K?
options: Returns the base extension of E to the degree-n extension of K
Returns n independent copies of E
Performs n iterations of point doubling
Returns the n-torsion subgroup of E
questions.explanation: BaseExtend(E, n) extends the base field to the degree-n extension of K.

questions.question: In which characteristic(s) does WeierstrassModel(E) NOT apply?
questions.explanation: A simplified Weierstrass model y² = x³ + ax + b may not exist in characteristic 2 or 3, so WeierstrassModel does not apply there.

questions.question: How many nonisomorphic twists over GF(13) does a curve of j-invariant 0 have, according to the handbook example?
questions.explanation: The example shows Twists(E3) returns 6 curves for a j-invariant 0 curve over GF(13), due to the nontrivial automorphism group.

questions.question: What does IsQuadraticTwist(E, F) return when E and F are quadratic twists of each other?
options: true and an element d such that F is isomorphic to QuadraticTwist(E, d)
true and the quadratic extension field over which they are isomorphic
questions.explanation: IsQuadraticTwist returns true together with a base-field element d witnessing the quadratic twist relationship.

questions.question: If integer coefficients are supplied when constructing an elliptic curve, into which ring are they automatically coerced?
questions.explanation: Integer coefficients are coerced into Q; the base ring of an elliptic curve must be a field.
